title: ulmus × hollandica var. pitteursii
intro: Ulmus × hollandica var. pitteursii, also known as the Dutch Elm tree, is a deciduous tree species that is a cross between the Wych Elm (Ulmus glabra) and the Field Elm (Ulmus minor). Traditionally, the bark of the Dutch Elm tree has been used for its medicinal properties, which include its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Ulmus × hollandica var. pitteursii include its ability to support the immune system, reduce fever, and alleviate symptoms of colds and flu. In herbal preparations, the bark of the tree can be made into a decoction or infusion, which can be used to treat a variety of health issues, including respiratory problems and skin conditions.
